verdict_reasoning: ADORA3 is a classical seven-transmembrane (7TM) G-protein-coupled receptor with an extracellular N-terminus and three extracellular loops. The adenosine-binding pocket is located in the extracellular domain formed by these loops and the N-terminal region, making the protein directly accessible to extracellular adenosine ligand. Adenosine receptors are well-characterized cell-surface receptors.
